report_name: ShinyHunters Claims Massive Canvas Breach, Disrupting 275 Million Users at 9,000 Institutions

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: Notorious hacking group that claimed responsibility for a catastrophic data breach of the Canvas Learning Management System (LMS). The group allegedly exfiltrated 3.65 TB of data and used public extortion tactics, including defacing login portals with a ransom note.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Used to gain initial access to the Canvas environment by exploiting a vulnerability in the 'Free-For-Teacher' account program.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: Used to exfiltrate a massive volume of data (3.65 TB) from Canvas data stores to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Internal Defacement
attack_patterns.description: Used to post ransom notes on public-facing Canvas login pages as a public extortion tactic to pressure the victim into paying.

vulnerabilities.name: Canvas Free-For-Teacher Program Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability related to the 'Free-For-Teacher' account program on the Canvas platform was exploited by ShinyHunters to gain initial access.

identities.name: Instructure
identities.description: The parent company of the Canvas Learning Management System (LMS) that was the primary victim of the data breach claimed by ShinyHunters.

identities.name: Educational Institutions
identities.description: A class of 8,809 educational institutions worldwide, including universities and schools, whose 275 million users' data was compromised in the Canvas breach.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Exfiltration Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled infrastructure used to receive the 3.65 TB of data exfiltrated from the Canvas LMS.

indicators.name: Anomalous Canvas Free-For-Teacher Account Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Canvas Free-For-Teacher Account Activity
id: 2b1c8e9f-4d5a-4f6b-8c7d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: 'Detects unusual login or API activity from accounts associated with the Canvas ''Free-For-Teacher'' program, which was identified as the initial access vector in a major breach.'
references:
  - https://www.cbc.ca/news/science/canvas-cyberattack-shinyhunters-1.7200213
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-10
tags:
  - attack.t1190
level: medium
logsource:
  category: application
  product: canvas
detection:
  keywords:
    - 'Free-For-Teacher'
    - 'account created'
    - 'logged in'
  condition: keywords
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate account creation and usage.
indicators.description: Detects activity related to the 'Free-For-Teacher' program accounts, which were the reported initial access vector.

indicators.name: Potential Canvas Data Enumeration or Exfiltration
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Canvas Data Enumeration or Exfiltration
id: 8d9e0f1a-2b3c-4d5e-6f7a-8b9c0d1e2f3a
status: experimental
description: 'Hunts for web server log requests to API endpoints that could indicate large-scale data enumeration or downloading, similar to the activity in the Canvas breach.'
references:
  - https://www.cbc.ca/news/science/canvas-cyberattack-shinyhunters-1.7200213
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-10
tags:
  - attack.t1567
level: medium
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    cs-method: 'GET'
    c-uri|startswith:
      - '/api/v1/accounts/'
      - '/api/v1/users/'
      - '/api/v1/courses/'
  timeframe: 10m
  condition: selection | count() > 1000
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts or data synchronization tools.
indicators.description: Detects unusually high volumes of API requests that could indicate data enumeration or exfiltration, as seen in the 3.65 TB data theft.

indicators.name: Canvas Ransom Note Defacement Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: Canvas Ransom Note Defacement Detection
id: 5c6d7e8f-9a0b-4c1d-2e3f-4a5b6c7d8e9f
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the specific ransom note text used by ShinyHunters to deface Canvas login portals.'
references:
  - https://www.govtech.com/education/higher-ed/some-canvas-users-receive-ransomware-threat-after-data-breach
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-10
tags:
  - attack.t1491.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection:
    Contents|contains: 'Make the right decision, don''t be the next headline.'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely, but could trigger on incident reports or articles discussing the note.
indicators.description: Detects the specific string from the ransom note used by ShinyHunters to deface login pages.

relationships.source_name: Potential Canvas Data Enumeration or Exfiltration
